title: NOVELMAN — A Web Novel Platform with AI at the Core
excerpt: Building a platform where AI doesn't replace the author — it amplifies the story. Architecture decisions, trade-offs, and lessons from building NOVELMAN.
body:
const{Fragment:e,jsx:t,jsxs:n}=arguments[0];function _createMdxContent(r){const i={code:"code",em:"em",h2:"h2",h3:"h3",li:"li",ol:"ol",p:"p",strong:"strong",ul:"ul",...r.components};return n(e,{children:[n(i.p,{children:["NOVELMAN started with a simple question: ",t(i.em,{children:"what if the reading experience was as considered as the writing experience?"})]}),"
",t(i.p,{children:"Most novel platforms are read-centric databases. The author uploads a file, readers page through chapters, ads run. NOVELMAN is designed differently: the AI is a collaborator in the authoring process, not an afterthought in the reader experience."}),"
",t(i.h2,{children:"The problem being solved"}),"
",t(i.p,{children:"Web novel authors — particularly indie authors — have three pain points:"}),"
",n(i.ol,{children:["
",n(i.li,{children:[t(i.strong,{children:"Consistency across long-form work"})," — character voice drift, plot thread management, world-state tracking"]}),"
",n(i.li,{children:[t(i.strong,{children:"Discovery"})," — small authors with good writing struggle to reach readers"]}),"
",n(i.li,{children:[t(i.strong,{children:"Reader retention"})," — static reading experiences don't compete well for attention"]}),"
"]}),"
",t(i.p,{children:"NOVELMAN targets all three, with AI as an enabling layer for the first and a better product design for the other two."}),"
",t(i.h2,{children:"Architecture"}),"
",t(i.p,{children:"The system is a Turborepo monorepo with three main apps:"}),"
",n(i.ul,{children:["
",n(i.li,{children:[t(i.code,{children:"apps/web"})," — public-facing reader app (Next.js, SSG for chapters)"]}),"
",n(i.li,{children:[t(i.code,{children:"apps/studio"})," — author writing and management environment"]}),"
",n(i.li,{children:[t(i.code,{children:"apps/api"})," — shared API (REST + WebSocket)"]}),"
"]}),"
",t(i.p,{children:"The AI layer uses a multi-agent orchestration pattern: separate agents for continuity checking, character consistency, and style analysis, coordinated by a central router."}),"
",t(i.h3,{children:"Database design"}),"
",t(i.p,{children:'The most interesting decision was the content schema. Chapters are stored as structured prose (not raw HTML), with metadata layers: scene tags, POV character, location, time marker. This structure is what enables the AI to reason about continuity — you can't ask "is this consistent with chapter 12" if chapter 12 is just a blob of text.'}),"
",t(i.h2,{children:"What I learned"}),"
",n(i.p,{children:[t(i.strong,{children:"Turborepo has sharp edges."})," Build configuration between packages takes more upfront investment than a monolith, but the development speed once set up is significantly better."]}),"
",n(i.p,{children:[t(i.strong,{children:"AI in authoring tools needs strict scoping."}),' Early versions of the AI were too "helpful" — they rewrote prose unprompted. The current design is strictly opt-in: the author triggers analysis, the AI responds, the author decides.']}),"
",n(i.p,{children:[t(i.strong,{children:"The reading experience is the product."})," The most impactful change wasn't the AI features — it was improving typography, reading progress, and offline capability. Readers notice quality before they notice features."]}),"
",t(i.h2,{children:"Current status"}),"
",n(i.p,{children:[t(i.code,{children:"[ACTIVE]"})," — In development. Core reading and writing flows are complete. AI continuity features are in beta. Discovery system is next."]})]})}return{default:function(e={}){const{wrapper:n}=e.components||{};return n?t(n,{...e,children:t(_createMdxContent,{...e})}):_createMdxContent(e)}};
